## Summary

What does this PR change, in one or two sentences?

## Type of change

- [ ] New checklist item(s)
- [ ] New guide or template
- [ ] Fix (broken link, typo, incorrect content)
- [ ] Interactive checklist (HTML/CSS/JS)
- [ ] Translation
- [ ] Other (describe below)

## Why

What problem does this solve, or what gap does it fill? (Skip this and a maintainer will ask anyway — every item in this kit ships with a "why," PRs should too.)

## Checklist

- [ ] I checked existing issues/PRs for duplicates
- [ ] New checklist items are atomic and include a one-line "why"
- [ ] Tone matches the existing guides (direct, specific, no fluff)
- [ ] If I touched `interactive-checklist.html`, I tested it in a real browser
- [ ] Links I added or changed actually resolve
